explanation: The requirement states that whenever the ventilator is operating in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) or PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, and if the GUI has set the Inspiratory Pause (i.e., the 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is true), there should be the possibility (i.e., it must be possible) to initiate an Inspiratory Pause (i.e., 'inspiratoryPause'). We interpret 'possibility' as an implication: if system is in one of those modes and button is set, then eventually it must be possible that inspiratoryPause is true. Thus, whenever (globally) we are in either PCVMode or PSVMode and inspiratoryPauseButton holds, eventually inspiratoryPause will hold.

explanation_dict.natural_language: System operates in PCV or PSV mode
The Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI
The Inspiratory Pause can be initiated
If at any time the mode is PCV or PSV and the button is set, then eventually Inspiratory Pause will be active
Globally, whenever this condition holds, the formula applies

explanation: The requirement states that in PCV or PSV modes, it must be possible to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if the GUI has set (requested) it. This expresses a possibility (not necessity) that when the mode is PCV or PSV and the user requests an Inspiratory Pause (via the GUI), then at some point, an Inspiratory Pause can actually be initiated. In LTL, 'PCVMode or PSVMode' can be written as (PCVMode | PSVMode), 'inspiratoryPauseButton' means the request is set by the GUI, and 'inspiratoryPause' means the pause has been actually initiated. The formula is: globally, whenever '(PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPauseButton' holds, it is possible (eventually) to reach a state where 'inspiratoryPause' holds (F inspiratoryPause). So, G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F inspiratoryPause).

explanation_dict.natural_language: In PCV or PSV mode
GUI requests Inspiratory Pause
Inspiratory Pause is initiated
Possibility to initiate if requested: if in mode and requested, then eventually possible to initiate
Globally (at all times)

explanation: First, we identify the key elements in the requirement. 'In PCV and PSV modes' means the formula applies when either PCVMode or PSVMode holds. 'There shall be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause' means that if the 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is activated (set by GUI), then 'inspiratoryPause' must be possible (i.e., will eventually hold). Putting these together, we say: whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) holds, globally, if inspiratoryPauseButton happens, then eventually inspiratoryPause must happen. In LTL, this is written as G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> (inspiratoryPauseButton -> F inspiratoryPause)), meaning: globally, in either PCV or PSV mode, if the pause button is pressed, then inspiratory pause will eventually be initiated.

explanation: The specification states that in PCV and PSV ventilation modes (represented by atomic propositions PCVMode and PSVMode), it must always be possible to initiate an inspiratory pause if the GUI has set it (inspiratoryPauseButton). In LTL, this is expressed as: whenever we are in either PCVMode or PSVMode, and the GUI has set the inspiratoryPauseButton, it is possible (eventually) to activate an inspiratoryPause. We formulate this as a global property: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton -> F inspiratoryPause). This means: in all states, if we are in PCV or PSV mode and the GUI has requested an inspiratory pause, then at some point in the future an inspiratory pause (inspiratoryPause) will occur.

explanation:
The requirement says: In PCV and PSV modes, there shall be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI. 

- 'In PCV and PSV modes' corresponds to: either PCVMode or PSVMode is active.
- 'there shall be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI' means: if the GUI requests an Inspiratory Pause via inspiratoryPauseButton, then in those modes, an inspiratoryPause may be initiated (i.e., it becomes true at some point).

We interpret 'possibility to initiate' as: whenever in PCVMode or PSVMode and inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed, then at some (future) point an inspiratoryPause is triggered (i.e., becomes true). This means: globally, in each moment, if we are in PCVMode or PSVMode and inspiratoryPauseButton, then eventually inspiratoryPause.

Hence, the formula is:

G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F inspiratoryPause )

inspiratory Pause may be initiated (it becomes true at some point)
if ... then ... (whenever in PCV/PSV and button is pressed, eventually inspiratoryPause)
Globally (always)

explanation:
The natural language sentence specifies that if the ventilator is operating in either PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) or PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, and if the GUI (Graphical User Interface) has set an Inspiratory Pause (by pressing the Inspiratory Pause button), then it should be possible to initiate an Inspiratory Pause. 

In LTL, this is formulated as: 
1. Detecting that you're in either PCV or PSV mode: (PCVMode | PSVMode)
2. The condition that 'the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI': inspiratoryPauseButton
3. The obligation: that it shall be possible to initiate an Inspiratory Pause (meaning the system will perform inspiratoryPause)

The requirement is for this to be true at all times whenever those antecedents are true, so we use the global (G) operator:
G( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton -> F inspiratoryPause )

This means: Globally, whenever the system is in PCV or PSV mode and the GUI triggers an Inspiratory Pause, eventually the system must initiate an Inspiratory Pause.

globally

explanation:
First, let's analyze the requirement: In PCV and PSV modes there shall be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI. 

Breaking it down:
- We need to express: 'In PCV or PSV mode, if the inspiratory pause is set via the GUI, then it must be possible to initiate inspiratoryPause.'
- 'PCVMode' and 'PSVMode' are the mode indicators.
- 'inspiratoryPauseButton' indicates the GUI request.
- 'inspiratoryPause' means the pause is actually initiated.

So, for all time, whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPauseButton holds, it should be possible to eventually get inspiratoryPause.
This is translated to: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F inspiratoryPause).
That is, globally, if (we are in PCV or PSV mode and the pause is set by the GUI), then eventually inspiratoryPause becomes active.

explanation: The requirement is: In PCV and PSV modes, there must be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI. 1) The phrase 'in PCV and PSV modes' is represented as (PCVMode | PSVMode). 2) 'there shall be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause' is interpreted as 'it shall be possible that inspiratoryPause' happens, i.e., it is possible in the future (F). 3) 'if it is set by the GUI' means if inspiratoryPauseButton is true, then this possibility must exist. 4) The structure is: Whenever we are in PCV or PSV mode, if the GUI sets the inspiratory pause, then it is eventually possible to make inspiratoryPause true. 5) We use the global (G) operator to state the property must always hold. So, the overall formula is: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> (inspiratoryPauseButton -> F inspiratoryPause)).

That is, whenever the ventilator is in either PCVMode or PSVMode, and the inspiratoryPauseButton is set (by the GUI), then it must be possible to activate inspiratoryPause. The formula is thus: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F inspiratoryPause).
